source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The cross section for electron impact excitation of a certain atomic level A is $\sigma_{A}=1.4 \times 10^{-20} \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$. The level has a lifetime \tau=2 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{sec}$, and decays 10 per cent of the time to level B and 90 per cent of the time to level C (Fig. 1.3). Calculate the light intensity emitted per \mathrm{cm}^{3}$ in the transition $A \rightarrow$ $B$, expressed in watts/steradian.
extraction_notes: images field contains a PIL image object string representation, not a file path or URL
question explicitly references Fig. 1.3 and record marks vision_relevance as essential

Calculate the light intensity emitted per $\\mathrm{cm}^{3}$ in the transition $A \rightarrow B$, expressed in watts/steradian.
